keyFindings: 2 of 3 gaps were predictable: clone and synchronize operations had full permission data in code-analysis but were excluded from task scope. ANTI-004 (enumeration without checklist) was violated.
Permission-requirement prose systematically triggers INV-style-132 ('must' directed at people) — 3 of 8 tasks rejected for this. New domain-specific anti-pattern candidate.
Research citation rate was 10% (1/10 approved recommendations cited). Low because task was prose gap-fill, not the how-to/API-reference work most sources target.
Knowledge brief over-included 5 entries (all correctly flagged as low applicability). Raising relevance threshold from 0.4 to 0.55 would eliminate over-inclusion without losing useful entries.
Hub page absorbing 44.7% of impacts predicted critical-risk multi-cycle outcome. Cross-ref tasks with ≤8 criteria predicted first-attempt acceptance.
